frontiers2017.title: Veronica officinalis Product Authentication Using DNA Metabarcoding and HPLC-MS Reveals Widespread Adulteration with Veronica chamaedrys

actions.action: Traditional expectorant for cough and respiratory complaints (iridoid glycosides inhibit lung inflammation)

constituents.name: Iridoid glycosides (aucubin, catalpol, verproside, verminoside)
constituents.note: Anti-inflammatory constituents that inhibit pro-inflammatory mediators in lung cells.

contraindications.note: Traditional use only; clinical evidence is limited, so a persistent cough or digestive complaint needs medical assessment. Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established.
Buy from a reputable source: Veronica officinalis is frequently adulterated with the related Veronica chamaedrys.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific and general literature (Frontiers in Pharmacology authentication/phytochemistry study; HerbaZest monograph). Speedwell is poorly covered by clinical trials, so most actions are needs-review. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, indigestion, skin-irritation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

uebel2020.author: Uebel, T., Hermes, L., Haupenthal, S., Muller, L. and Esselen, M.
uebel2020.title: alpha-Asarone, beta-asarone, and gamma-asarone: Current status of toxicological evaluation

he2023.author: He, X., Chen, X., Yang, Y., Liu, Y. and Xie, Y.
he2023.title: Acorus calamus var. angustatus Besser: Insight into current research on ethnopharmacological use, phytochemistry, pharmacology, toxicology, and pharmacokinetics

actions.action: Aromatic bitter / carminative for indigestion, bloating, flatulence and poor appetite (traditional)
Traditional nervine for memory and concentration (preclinical neuroprotective activity)

constituents.name: Phenylpropanoid asarones (alpha- and beta-asarone)
constituents.note: Characteristic aromatic constituents - but beta-asarone is the toxic, safety-limiting compound.

constituents.name: Essential oil and lignans
constituents.note: Other aromatic and bioactive constituents of the rhizome.

contraindications.note: SERIOUS: beta-asarone is hepatotoxic, mutagenic and carcinogenic in animal studies. Regulators have restricted internal use of asarone-containing Acorus calamus (the Asian high-beta-asarone varieties), and it is banned as a food additive in the USA. Avoid internal use except properly characterised beta-asarone-free preparations.
Do not use in pregnancy or breastfeeding, or for prolonged periods. The North American variety (Acorus americanus) is essentially beta-asarone-free, but most commercial calamus is not.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Journal of Applied Toxicology asarone toxicology review; Phytochemistry systematic review). Record status set to needs-review because beta-asarone carries documented carcinogenicity and regulatory restrictions. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (indigestion, bloating, appetite-loss); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

rxlist.author: RxList
rxlist.title: Sweet Violet: Health Benefits, Side Effects, Uses, Dose & Precautions

actions.action: Demulcent / expectorant for cough, bronchitis and catarrh (mucilage soothes; saponins loosen mucus)
Mild sedative for sleeplessness and to ease headache (traditional)

constituents.name: Mucilage and saponins
constituents.note: Mucilage gives the soothing demulcent action; saponins give the expectorant action.

contraindications.note: Generally well tolerated as flower/leaf preparations. It contains salicylates, so use caution if you are salicylate-sensitive; the root in large doses is emetic and purgative.
Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established, so avoid medicinal doses.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from general phytotherapy literature (Herbal Reality and RxList monographs). Note: distinct from the book's Viola tricolor (heartsease). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, insomnia, sore-throat, headache); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Thymus vulgaris L. and Thymus zygis L., herba (Thymi herba)

sharifirad2018.author: Sharifi-Rad, M., Varoni, E.M., Iriti, M., Martorell, M., Setzer, W.N., del Mar Contreras, M., Salehi, B., Soltani-Nejad, A., Rajabi, S., Tajbakhsh, M. and Sharifi-Rad, J.
sharifirad2018.title: Carvacrol and human health: A comprehensive review

actions.action: Expectorant for productive (chesty) cough associated with cold and bronchitis
Antitussive and antispasmodic (eases bronchial spasm and coughing)
Antimicrobial (thymol and carvacrol) - supports respiratory infection

constituents.name: Essential oil rich in thymol and carvacrol
constituents.note: Phenolic monoterpenes responsible for the antimicrobial and expectorant actions.

contraindications.note: Culinary amounts are safe. Thyme essential oil is a strong irritant - dilute well before any topical use and do not take the undiluted oil internally.

A cough that persists beyond about a week, or with fever or breathlessness, needs medical assessment. Avoid medicinal/oil doses in pregnancy; allergy is possible in people sensitive to the mint family (Lamiaceae).

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and scientific literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Thymi herba; Phytotherapy Research carvacrol review). Note: this is Thymus vulgaris (garden thyme), distinct from the book's Thymus serpyllum (Breckland thyme). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, cold, sore-throat, bronchitis, infection); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Potentilla erecta (L.) Raeusch., rhizoma (Tormentillae rhizoma)

actions.action: Astringent for mild diarrhoea (ellagitannins tighten the gut lining)
Anti-inflammatory gargle / mouthwash for sore throat and inflammation of the mouth and throat
Supportive in inflammatory bowel disease / ulcerative colitis (limited trial evidence)

constituents.name: Tannins (ellagitannins such as agrimoniin, and catechin-type tannins)

contraindications.note: Tannin-rich: high or prolonged intake may cause stomach upset or constipation and can reduce the absorption of iron and some medicines taken at the same time.
Diarrhoea persisting more than 3-4 days, or with fever or blood in the stool, needs medical care. Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended in the absence of data.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and phytotherapy literature (EMA HMPC and ESCOP monographs for Tormentillae rhizoma). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (diarrhea, sore-throat, ibd); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
